fieldValues.value: RFC-017 Change A: Add `attaches` to the `SourceReference.sourceRole` enum
All four SourceReference-bearing schemas: record.json, note.json, typed-record.json, relations-collection.json

Add `"attaches"` as a new value in the `SourceReference.sourceRole` closed enum. The enum expands from `["evidence", "extracted-from", "quoted-from", "inspired-by"]` to include `"attaches"`. Each of the four SourceReference-bearing schemas carries its own local `$defs.SourceReference` (not a shared `$ref`) and all four must be updated identically. An attachment is a `SourceReference` with `sourceType: "repository-document"`, `sourceRole: "attaches"`, and `sourceId` equal to the target's `documentId`. Attachment is not modelled as a `Relation` edge; no new canonical Relation type and no `com.semanticops.srs` package version bump. The `attaches` value satisfies RFC-023 invariant I-88 (sourceRole/Relation vocabulary disjointness).

A `Relation` addresses its target via `targetInstanceId`; a source document is not an instance and has no `instanceId`. The `SourceReference` mechanism already exists to link a record to a source document by `documentId` and is the honest home for the attachment link. Reusing `sourceRefs` with a new `sourceRole` value adds no new field, avoids a category error (not forcing a document into `instanceIndex`), and delivers two properties the attachment model depends on: references are minted only through the core import path, and they are durable independent of content presence.
